en.extract:
The great Inca finch (Incaspiza pulchra) is a species of passerine bird in the tanager family Thraupidae. It was traditionally placed in the family Emberizidae. It is endemic to Peru.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland.

The '''great Inca finch''' ('''''Incaspiza pulchra''''') is a [[species]] of [[passerine]] bird in the tanager family [[Thraupidae]]. It was traditionally placed in the family [[Emberizidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to Peru.

Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland.
[[File:GreatInca-Finch.jpg|thumb|left|Santa Eulalia Valley - Peru]]
